analysis_description: Round-3 tapes days 0–2: Black–Scholes implied vol vs m_t=log(K/S)/sqrt(T) with T from round3description (CSV day 0,1,2 → 8,7,6 d at open) and intraday DTE wind-down (timestamps 0..999900 step 100 → ~1 day per session) per round3work/plotting/original_method/combined_analysis/plot_iv_smile_round3.py. Fitted quadratic IV(m_t) per subsample; mean quadratic coefficient a>0 (upward curvature); mean wing IV − core IV > 0; bid-ask width on VEV_5200 and extract mids from CSV.
used_to_decide: Positive mean IV quadratic term and wing−core IV premium support fading rich wings vs smooth core; spread filters sized vs ~3-tick VEV and ~5-tick extract widths. Chose v0 thresholds then refined cheap-wing filter in v2 after v1 sweep hurt PnL.

analysis_description: Implicit grid over thresholds / hedge / m_t filter (trader_v1 vs v0 backtests); confirmed full hedge with tight |m_t| gate reduced wing shorting too much and worsened aggregate PnL versus v0.
used_to_decide: Steered v2 back toward v0 structure with IV mispricing guard (time-value ratio) instead of m_t-only gate.

analysis_description: Compared v2 vs v0 PnL decomposition in backtest logs: large losses on VEV_4500 longs in v0; v2’s time-value filter reduced extract hedge bleed while keeping wing shorts.
used_to_decide: Kept v2 as preferred active curvature implementation for next tuning.

analysis_description: v3 sell-only sweep: smile residuals rarely exceeded lowered RICH threshold with spread filter; activity collapsed to incidental position on VEV_6000.
used_to_decide: Sell-only at this threshold is too passive; future iterations should blend sell-rich with selective inversion or lower RICH_THR with care for fill quality.

analysis_description: Per-strike IV/Greek tradeability study on ROUND_3 tapes (subsample every 100 ticks): BS implied vol from mids (r=0), per-timestamp quadratic IV(m_t) residuals, plus delta/vega summaries by strike. DTE/TTE mapping from round3description interpreted as historical CSV day 0/1/2 -> 8/7/6 at open, with intraday wind-down ~1 day over the session.
used_to_decide: Found VEV_4000/4500 have near-zero time-value fraction and noisier residuals, while 5400/5500 retain meaningful vega and tradable residual structure; informed focused-wing variants v4/v5 and lower-wing long suppression in v6.

analysis_description: Variant comparison on same curvature thesis: v4 (focused 5400/5500 with short-cover) was too lossy due to hedge bleed; v5 (stricter + no cheap-wing longs) turned fully passive; v6 (active v2-parent with lower-wing long suppression) remained active but underperformed.
used_to_decide: Best realized result remains conservative/passy v5 at total 0, but that confirms current residual triggers are too weak under worse fills; next work should re-center residual estimation/entry timing while keeping tradeability filters.

analysis_description: v6 vs v2: blocking 4000/4500 cheap longs reduced some loss tail but total PnL worsened vs v2; still valid as a parent for strike-scoped experiments.
used_to_decide: Preferred parent for next grid remained v2 (−751) over v6 (−2326).

analysis_description: Signed residual check on tapes (50-tick step, time-value filter): IV−quadratic(m_t) at 5400/5500 is almost always negative (short side of parabola); 6000 often positive. Same TTE mapping as round3description + intraday wind-down. Correlation artifact: analyze_wing_resid_vs_extract_v7.py → wing_resid_vs_extract_v7.json (Δlog S vs Δresid, weak pooled r ~0.05–0.09 for 5400).
used_to_decide: Explains v7–v10 zero activity when entry required resid>RICH on 5400/5500 only; led to v11 signed-strike split (short 6000 rich, long 5400/5500 cheap).

analysis_description: n/a — v8 grid vs v7 (same structural issue).
n/a — v9 lower RICH/EXIT on 5400/5500-only short rule.
used_to_decide: Still no positive RICH events at 5400/5500 at scale under quadratic fit.

analysis_description: n/a — v10 lower-threshold grid vs v8.
v11 backtest: signed-strike book triggered but total −3357 (extract hedge drag). v2 still best in-folder; pivot documented without abandoning smile-residual concept.
used_to_decide: Next iterations should branch from v2: strike-specific or cubic smile, or smaller option clips / lower HEDGE_FRAC to reduce extract churn.

analysis_description: analyze_quad_vs_cubic_v12.py → quad_vs_cubic_smile_v12.json: cubic IV(m_t) lowers in-sample RMSE vs quadratic but mean |resid| on wing strikes is larger; with v2’s fixed RICH/CHEAP, v12 never entered (0 PnL).
used_to_decide: Kept quadratic for v13–v15 parameter sweeps; cubic would need threshold recalibration.

analysis_description: v13 vs v2: lower HEDGE_FRAC (0.62) and MAX_LEG 10 cut extract bleed; total +759 vs v2 −751 on days 0–2 (worse-match backtest).
used_to_decide: Confirmed hedge as main loss channel; extended to v15.

analysis_description: v14 HEDGE 0.50 vs v13 0.62: int-rounded extract hedge matched v13 path in this run (same total 759).
used_to_decide: Use hedge fractions that change int(round(hedge*d_net)) when grid-searching.

analysis_description: v15 HEDGE 0.28: option legs match v13 pattern but extract PnL mostly near zero (small d_net rounds to 0 hedge), total 3010 on days 0–2 — new best in this strategy folder.
used_to_decide: Prefer v15 line for next micro-sweeps (RICH/CHEAP, optional cap on extract hedge when |d_net| large).
